_disclaimer: Informational reference data only. Not legal, tax, or financial advice. Verify with qualified professionals before relying on any values. Current as of March 2026.
title: Recommended Action Rules
description: Declarative rules for generating recommended estate actions. Each rule describes a condition and the action to recommend when the condition is met.

rules.title: Get professional valuation for high-value asset
rules.description: Assets estimated over £50,000 should have a professional valuation for tax purposes.

rules.title: Update stale valuation
rules.description: Valuations older than 2 years may not reflect current market value and should be refreshed.

rules.title: Obtain property survey or formal appraisal
rules.description: Real property should have a professional survey or appraisal for accurate estate valuation.

rules.title: Complete lifetime transfer documentation within lookback period
rules.description: Lifetime transfers within the jurisdiction's lookback period must be fully documented for accurate tax calculation.

rules.title: Add death benefit nomination to pension
rules.description: Pensions without a nomination expression of wish form may result in benefits being paid at the scheme trustees' discretion.

rules.title: Designate beneficiary on insurance policy
rules.description: Insurance policies without a designated beneficiary may form part of the estate and be subject to tax.

rules.title: Consider writing insurance policy into trust
rules.description: Insurance policies not written in trust form part of the estate for tax purposes. Writing them into trust can reduce the taxable estate.

rules.title: Appoint at least one executor
rules.description: An estate without a named executor will require the court to appoint an administrator, causing delays and additional cost.

rules.title: Appoint guardian for minor children
rules.description: If the testator has minor children and no guardian is appointed, the court will decide guardianship.

rules.title: Record will execution date
rules.description: The will execution date is required to establish which version of the will is current and valid.

rules.title: Create disposal plan for urgent asset
rules.description: Assets flagged as urgent (e.g., perishable goods, time-limited investments) need a documented disposal or realization plan.

rules.title: Calculate estate tax position
rules.description: A tax position should be calculated to understand the estate's potential tax liability and identify planning opportunities.

rules.title: Improve estate completeness score
rules.description: The estate completeness score is below 50%. Review the completeness checklist and address outstanding items.

rules.title: Obtain death certificate
rules.description: A death certificate is required before probate can be applied for and before most estate administration tasks can proceed.

rules.title: Apply for probate / letters of administration
rules.description: The estate has been in pre-probate status for over 3 months. Probate or letters of administration should be applied for promptly.

rules.title: Review lifetime transfer for applicable exemptions
rules.description: This lifetime transfer has no exemptions claimed. Review whether annual exemption, small gifts exemption, or other reliefs apply.

rules.title: Add contact details for beneficiary
rules.description: Beneficiaries need contact details on file so they can be notified during estate administration.

rules.title: Record ownership details for co-owned asset
rules.description: Co-owned assets need ownership shares documented to determine the portion that forms part of the estate.
